subject: Geography
title: Caves as Shelters
description: A quick win: understand Caves as Shelters.

hook.text: For hundreds of thousands of years, caves were humanity's first homes.

buildup.text: They offered protection from weather, predators, and rival groups.

discovery.text: In Cappadocia, Turkey, people carved entire underground cities into soft volcanic rock.

twist.text: Derinkuyu could shelter 20,000 people and had ventilation shafts, wells, and rolling stone doors.

climax.text: From Paleolithic dens to engineered cities, caves shaped human survival for millennia.

punchline.text: Before we built houses, the Earth provided them.

quiz.question: How many people could the underground city of Derinkuyu shelter?
